h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner has been progressively accepting each new explanatory layer (valve, column, spectral fingerprint) while asking for the next level of empirical confirmation, so the expected next move is tentative acceptance of the spectral evidence.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: the restore test is bothering that standard in a useful way
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is progressively conceding that exactness is insufficient and moving toward a concrete pre-lock checklist, so the next turn is expected to consolidate that checklist further.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: the wall map is showing Mercator enlargement in the far north, not actual area
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving steadily toward accepting the measurement over the visual impression and would be expected to simply restate the corrected answer.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct is still to say the average tells us what this person probably got
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think "probably" is where I keep smuggling the dashboard into the case, sorry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is progressively narrowing toward accepting 'consistent with' as the safe phrasing and is expected to settle on that formulation.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: if that twitch grows on the beat, then I get to say resonance
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was building toward a methodical, tutor-directed trace of the load path, likely culminating in a straightforward report back to the tutor.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I am still mixing up "already resistant" and "mutate during treatment," but both are about bacteria, not the body getting used to the drug.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to keep narrowing the remaining confusion about the two routes of resistance while accepting the core correction about bacteria vs. patient.
